methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series G → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.25x) to $6.0B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Drones & Autonomous (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 2 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 3 same-sector peers exist in Drones & Autonomous, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Zipline
private.rationale: Same sector (Drones & Autonomous), same stage (Series G)

data.narrative:
Nuro is a Series G autonomous delivery drone company founded by Dave Ferguson and Jiajun Zhu, currently valued at $6.0B post-money and having raised $2.84B to date. The Drones & Autonomous sector contains four known private peers—Joby Aviation, Archer Aviation, Elroy Air, and Zipline—and no public comparables, limiting the available comp universe for traditional benchmarking.

Only one valuation method proved applicable to this analysis. Stage-progression modeling, grounded in Nuro's $6.0B Series G post-money valuation, applies a 1.25x step-up multiplier typical for progression to the next funding stage, yielding an implied valuation of $7.5B. A peer-median approach was not feasible; while three same-sector peers exist, none satisfied the filters for same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-tier comparables—the narrow private market in autonomous delivery drones lacks sufficient density of comparable data at Nuro's scale.

The analysis supports a range of $6.4B to $8.6B, with a median of $7.5B. This range reflects a 6–44% upside from the current $6.0B valuation, anchored primarily on stage-progression mechanics rather than market-based peer triangulation. Key uncertainties include the scarcity of precedent transactions in early-stage autonomous delivery at this valuation tier and structural differences between Nuro's ground-delivery model and the eVTOL and last-mile peers in the broader sector. Validation through follow-on financing activity or comparable exit data would strengthen conviction.
